title: Getting started with admixr2
text: admixr2 fits pharmacometric PK/PD models summary-level data. clinical study supply: E — observed mean vector (one entry per observation time) V — observed covariance matrix (variance vector) n — sample size times — observation time points ev — dosing event table estimators match E V model-predicted counterparts return standard nlmixr2 fit object. lets apply established nlmixr2 models aggregate statistics publications internal data summaries individual records unavailable. Four estimators available: adfo natural starting point model screening initial estimates. admc workhorse standard PK models. adgh noise-free alternative admc models ~4 etas. adirmc preferred complex ODE systems expensive solves, high-dimensional IIV, poor starting values. See vignette("estimator-comparison", package = "admixr2") detailed comparison.

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: examplomycin ships admixr2: 500 simulated subjects two-compartment PK model first-order absorption (100 mg oral dose, sampled 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 12 h). True parameters: CL = 5 L/h, V1 = 10 L, V2 = 30 L, Q = 10 L/h, ka = 1 h⁻¹; IIV = 0.3 (SD log scale) parameters; proportional error SD = 0.2.

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Reshape individual records subjects × times matrix, compute E V: V 9×9 sample covariance matrix. -diagonal entries capture within-subject correlation across time; using full matrix (method = "cov") typically tightens parameter estimates compared diagonal-approximation (method = "var"). admixr2 auto-detects method structure V.
code: obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) n     <- length(ids)                     # 500  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] }  E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov.wt(dv_mat, method = "ML")$cov  round(E, 2) #> [1] 0.97 1.94 2.79 3.02 2.26 1.65 1.06 0.75 0.51

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Models use standard nlmixr2 syntax mu-referenced log-scale parameters: Writing parameter exp(tcl + eta.cl) called mu-referencing: structural fixed effect random effect enter additively log scale. admixr2 exploits pairing compute analytical gradients via sensitivity equations. See Advanced usage vignette details, including parameters without random effect handled.
code: pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Bundle study’s statistics named list:
code: study <- list(   E     = E,   V     = V,                       # full 9x9 covariance matrix   n     = n,   times = times,   ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 100)    # single 100 mg oral dose )

title: Getting started with admixr2
text: Pass one named studies admControl():
code: fit <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies   = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim     = 5000L,     cov_n_sim = 10000L,     maxeval   = 300L,     seed      = 1L   ) )

title: Advanced usage
text: study’s V determines branch NLL used. admixr2 auto-detects method: Override setting method explicitly study list: Use "cov" full covariance matrix expect temporal correlation inform parameter estimates. Use "var" marginal variances available (e.g. published table means SDs) runtime priority.
code: # Force full covariance NLL even for a diagonal V study_cov <- c(study, list(method = "cov"))  # Diagonal approximation when you only have marginal variances study_var <- list(   E      = E,   V      = diag(diag(V)),   # drop off-diagonal entries   n      = n,   times  = times,   ev     = rxode2::et(amt = 100),   method = "var" )

title: Advanced usage
text: admControl() adfoControl() offer gradient strategies via grad argument. adghControl() offers "analytical", "fd", "cfd", "none". adirmcControl() offers "analytical", "none", "fd" ("sens" "cfd"). admControl() gradient modes: adfoControl() gradient modes: adghControl() gradient modes: GH objective noise-free (deterministic quadrature, MC draws), analytical gradient exact resulting Hessian well-conditioned — "analytical" recommended default adgh. adfo, gradient modes still use sensitivity model inside NLL evaluation compute J single rxSolve — grad controls optimizer gradient formed. "sens" recommended standard ODE linCmt() models. sensitivity model unavailable (e.g. rare ODE features), admixr2 falls back "fd" automatically warning.
code: # Analytical gradient (default; fastest for ODE models) fit_sens <- nlmixr2(pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(studies = list(s = study), grad = "sens",                        n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L))  # Derivative-free — ignores maxeval; use nloptr stopping criteria instead fit_bobyqa <- nlmixr2(pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(studies = list(s = study), grad = "none",                        n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L))

title: Advanced usage
text: Mu-referencing nlmixr2 convention expressing structural parameter fixed effect plus random effect known back-transformation: admixr2 uses pairing classify parameters three cases, handled differently grad = "sens": Paired (mu-referenced) parameters — rxode2 augments ODE system sensitivity equations d(pred)/d(eta_i) random effect. tcl eta.cl enter additively log scale, gradient NLL respect tcl equals gradient respect eta.cl. recovered single ODE solve, extra function evaluations. Non-mu-referenced parameters — structural parameter random effect written separately (e.g. cl <- exp(tcl) * exp(eta.cl)), admixr2 recover d(NLL)/d(tcl) sensitivity equations alone. sensitivity model still used etas, omega, sigma; unpaired structural parameters require targeted CRN finite difference solve. warning issued grad = "sens" kept. Parameters without random effect — structural parameters corresponding eta (e.g. v2 <- exp(tv2) IIV dropped V2) always handled CRN finite differences. perturbation uses quasi-random seed nominal draw Monte Carlo noise largely cancels difference.
code: cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)   # mu-referenced: tcl paired with eta.cl

title: Advanced usage
text: IRMC estimator shifts weights moving proposal mean structural parameter changes. works directly mu-referenced parameters (e.g. cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)) weight shift closed-form function back-transformed ratio. non-mu-referenced parameters — structural thetas without paired random effect (e.g. ka <- exp(tka) eta.ka) — changing tka inner optimisation alters population mean prediction f(theta, 0) without corresponding weight path. kappa correction accounts adding kappa = f(theta_cand, 0) - f(theta_outer, 0) predicted mean, keeping inner NLL anchored true prediction candidate point. Kappa zero struct thetas mu-referenced. Two methods control f(theta_cand, 0) evaluated inner optimisation: "exact" (default): re-evaluates f(theta_cand, 0) via single rxSolve inner NLL evaluation. Exact; costs one extra rxSolve per inner step. "linearized": precomputes J = df/d(theta) per outer iteration via small FD batch. Approximates kappa_fn(theta_cand) ≈ f0 + J %*% (theta_cand - theta0) — pure arithmetic per inner step, zero extra rxSolve calls. "exact" accurate adds rxSolve every inner NLL call. "linearized" faster approximation good inner steps stay small relative outer box constraint — typical converged phases. Prefer "linearized" complex ODE models rxSolve expensive.
code: adirmcControl(..., kappa_method = "exact")       # default adirmcControl(..., kappa_method = "linearized")  # faster for complex ODE models

title: Advanced usage
text: Multi-restart fitting guards local optima. workers > 1 runs restarts parallel; admixr2 manages worker pool internally: cores distributed automatically: floor(total_cores / n_workers) threads per worker, remainder allocated cyclically (e.g. 13 cores / 4 workers → 4, 3, 3, 3). Workers stopped restart phase cores freed covariance Hessian step. Call admStopWorkers() manually fit interrupted cleanup.
code: fit_par <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies    = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim      = 5000L,     n_restarts = 4L,     workers    = 4L,      # background worker processes (mirai daemons)     cores      = 8L,      # total rxSolve OpenMP threads across workers     restart_sd = 0.3,     # SD of log-scale perturbation from the starting point     seed       = 1L   ) )

title: Advanced usage
text: sampling argument controls eta samples drawn: Sobol sequences typically require 2–5× fewer samples plain normal draws equivalent NLL variance. small n_sim, "lhs" provides better coverage Sobol.

title: Advanced usage
text: covMethod = "r" (default) computes numerical Hessian optimisation reports standard errors print(fit). Important: SEs computed structural residual-error parameters ; omega (IIV) SEs computed (matching nlmixr2 FOCEI behavior). larger cov_n_sim reduces MC noise Hessian: Set covMethod = "none" skip uncertainty estimation early model development need point estimates: Hessian non-positive-definite (SEs printed NA), increase cov_h_outer cov_n_sim.
code: fit_cov <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies     = list(examplomycin = study),     n_sim       = 5000L,     cov_n_sim   = 10000L,   # more samples → lower Hessian noise     cov_h_outer = 2.5e-3,   # outer FD step scale (default: eps^(1/5) ≈ 2.5e-3)     covMethod   = "r",     seed        = 1L   ) ) admControl(..., covMethod = "none")

title: Advanced usage
text: Standard information criteria work directly admFit objects. AIC BIC comparable across estimators evaluate likelihood. admc, adgh, adirmc target exact aggregate MVN likelihood (MC, quadrature, importance-weighted estimates integral), information criteria mutually comparable. adfo evaluates linearised likelihood differs , must compared three. compare full model (IIV five parameters) reduced model IIV CL V1 : Lower AIC/BIC favours parsimonious model; difference > 10 generally considered strong evidence.

title: Generating aggregate data with datagen
text: Two common situations call generating aggregate data programmatically: Simulation studies. want check estimator recovers true parameters. Define data-generating model known parameters, call datagen() obtain E V, fit analysis model, compare estimates truth. Literature-based modelling. published study analysed structural PK model (possibly different number compartments, different parameterisation, subset IIV terms). datagen() lets study carry model simulated aggregate data reflects original authors’ model, single shared structure. cases output named list (E, V, n, times, ev) objects plug directly admControl(studies = ...).

SE   %RSE #> tcl           Log clearance (L/h)    1.608 0.009336 0.5806 #> tv                 Log volume (L)    2.303  0.01276 0.5543 #> tka     Log absorption rate (1/h) 0.001742  0.01798   1032 #> prop.sd     Proportional error SD   0.1999                 #>         Back-transformed(95%CI) BSV(CV%) Shrink(SD)% #> tcl        4.994 (4.903, 5.086)     30.4             #> tv            10 (9.754, 10.25)     19.6             #> tka       1.002 (0.9671, 1.038)     20.9             #> prop.sd                  0.1999                      #>   #>   Covariance Type (fit_sim$covMethod): r #>   No correlations in between subject variability (BSV) matrix #>   Full BSV covariance (fit_sim$omega)  #>     or correlation (fit_sim$omegaR; diagonals=SDs) #>   Distribution stats (mean/skewness/kurtosis/p-value) available in $shrink  #>   Censoring (fit_sim$censInformation): No censoring #>   Minimization message (fit_sim$message):   #>     NLOPT_XTOL_REACHED: Optimization stopped because xtol_rel or xtol_abs (above) was reached. est   <- fit_sim$env$admExtra$struct truth <- c(tcl = log(5), tv = log(10), tka = log(1))  data.frame(   parameter = names(truth),   truth     = round(exp(truth), 2),   estimate  = round(exp(est[names(truth)]), 2) ) #>     parameter truth estimate #> tcl       tcl     5     4.99 #> tv         tv    10    10.00 #> tka       tka     1     1.00

title: Generating aggregate data with datagen
text: digitising data publications, study comes model. 2016 paper may used one-compartment model; 2022 follow-may used two compartments. datagen() accepts model element inside study spec overrides top-level default: studies share CL (5 L/h) differ describe distribution: earlier study lumped peripheral distribution single larger apparent volume (V = 15 L), later study resolved two compartments (V₁ = 10 L, V₂ = 30 L, Q = 10 L/h). two studies share drug (CL) different E profiles different dosing distributional assumptions: Simulated aggregate profiles two publications different structural models.
code: # One-compartment oral model (as published in a simpler earlier study) model_1cmt <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)     tv      <- log(40)    # apparent volume, peripheral compartment lumped in     tka     <- log(1)     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.25)     eta.cl  ~ 0.04     eta.v   ~ 0.01     eta.ka  ~ 0.01   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v  <- exp(tv  + eta.v)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)   <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central) <- ka * depot - (cl/v) * central     cp <- central / v     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  # Two-compartment oral model (as published in a more detailed later study) model_2cmt <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)     tv1     <- log(10)     tv2     <- log(30)     tq      <- log(10)     tka     <- log(1)     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)     eta.cl  ~ 0.09     eta.v1  ~ 0.04   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2)     q  <- exp(tq)     ka <- exp(tka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) } lit_data <- datagen(   studies = list(     early_study = list(       model = model_1cmt,       times = c(0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12),       ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 100),       n     = 120L     ),     later_study = list(       model = model_2cmt,       times = c(2, 4, 8, 12, 24),       ev    = rxode2::et(amt = 200),       n     = 180L     )   ),   control = datagenControl(n_sim = 10000L, seed = 2L) ) df_lit <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(lit_data), function(nm) {   s  <- lit_data[[nm]]   sd <- sqrt(diag(s$V))   data.frame(study = nm, time = s$times,              mean = s$E, lo = s$E - sd, hi = s$E + sd) }))  ggplot(df_lit, aes(x = time, y = mean, colour = study, fill = study)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2.5) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(early_study = "#009E73", later_study = "#CC79A7")) +   scale_fill_manual(  values = c(early_study = "#009E73", later_study = "#CC79A7")) +   labs(title  = "Literature-derived aggregate profiles",        x = "Time (h)", y = "Concentration (mg/L)",        colour = NULL, fill = NULL) +   theme_bw()

what: Fitting an analysis model to literature data
title: Generating aggregate data with datagen
text: data-generating models encode publication’s assumptions; analysis model represents unified structural hypothesis. fit two-compartment oral model studies jointly: two studies used genuinely different structural models, analysis model find parameter values minimise joint discrepancy predictions studies’ aggregate data — weighted compromise across two parameterisations. expected behaviour: aggregate-data modelling accepts study published different structural assumption seeks unified set parameters consistent .
code: lit_analysis <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)     tv1     <- log(10)     tv2     <- log(30)     tq      <- log(10)     tka     <- log(1)     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.25)     eta.cl  ~ 0.09     eta.v1  ~ 0.04   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2)     q  <- exp(tq)     ka <- exp(tka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  fit_lit <- nlmixr2(   lit_analysis, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies   = lit_data,     n_sim     = 5000L,     cov_n_sim = 10000L,     seed      = 3L   ) )

title: Generating aggregate data with datagen

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: study observed data mean vector y‾\bar{y} covariance matrix SS computed nn subjects. multivariate normal approximation aggregate log-likelihood −2ℓ=n[log|Vpred|+tr(Vpred−1S)+r⊤Vpred−1r],r=y‾−μpred -2\ell = n \left[   \log |V_\text{pred}| +   \operatorname{tr}(V_\text{pred}^{-1} S) +   r^\top V_\text{pred}^{-1} r \right], \qquad r = \bar{y} - \mu_\text{pred} μpred\mu_\text{pred} VpredV_\text{pred} model-predicted population mean covariance observations. nonlinear model f(θ,η)f(\theta,\eta) integrals random-effects distribution η∼𝒩(0,Ω)\eta \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Omega): μpred=𝔼η[f(θ,η)],Vpred=Var⁡η[f(θ,η)]+Σ \mu_\text{pred} = \mathbb{E}_\eta[f(\theta, \eta)], \qquad V_\text{pred} = \operatorname{Var}_\eta[f(\theta, \eta)] + \Sigma integrals closed form nonlinear ff. four estimators minimise objective take different approaches evaluating .

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: adfo sidesteps integral entirely approximating f(θ,η)f(\theta, \eta) first-order Taylor expansion around η=0\eta = 0: f(θ,η)≈f(θ,0)+Jη,Jt,j=∂ft∂ηj|η=0 f(\theta, \eta) \approx f(\theta, 0) + J\,\eta, \qquad J_{t,j} = \left.\frac{\partial f_t}{\partial \eta_j}\right|_{\eta=0} Substituting population moments gives closed-form expressions: μpred=f(θ,0),Vpred=JΩJ⊤+Σ \mu_\text{pred} = f(\theta, 0), \qquad V_\text{pred} = J\,\Omega\,J^\top + \Sigma Jacobian JJ obtained single rxSolve call via sensitivity equations augment ODE system ∂f/∂ηj\partial f / \partial \eta_j — finite differences, extra solves per eta. makes adfo fastest estimator: one rxSolve per NLL evaluation, fully deterministic. approximation holds: Taylor expansion exact ff linear η\eta. nonlinear models large IIV true population covariance exceeds JΩJ⊤J\Omega J^\top; FO underestimates Ω\Omega bias grows degree nonlinearity. AIC note: adfo evaluates linearised likelihood. objective scale admc adirmc must used cross-estimator AIC comparisons.

what: Monte Carlo (MC): sample average over η\eta
title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: admc estimates population integrals directly drawing NN samples ηi∼𝒩(0,Ω)\eta_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Omega) computing sample moments: μpred=1N∑=1Nf(θ,ηi),Vpred=1N∑=1N(f(θ,ηi)−μpred)(f(θ,ηi)−μpred)⊤+Σ \mu_\text{pred} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{=1}^N f(\theta, \eta_i), \qquad V_\text{pred} = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{=1}^N   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_i) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_i) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)^\top + \Sigma approximation made ff — estimator asymptotically exact N→∞N \\infty. Sobol quasi-random sequences (default) reduce MC variance relative plain normal draws, typically requiring 2–5× fewer samples equivalent precision. key cost every NLL evaluation requires NN rxSolve calls — one per sample. analytical gradient (sensitivity equations mu-referenced parameters; common random numbers FD otherwise) adds solves, base cost per optimizer step remains N×N \times (cost one rxSolve). Advantages: Asymptotically exact; AIC directly comparable across admc fits. Works well standard 1–2 compartment PK models moderate IIV. Limitations: NLL evaluation requires NN rxSolve calls; slower adfo. MC noise gradient can cause optimiser oscillation low n_sim.

what: Iterative Reweighting MC (IRMC): proposals fixed, inner loop free
title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: adirmc addresses main bottleneck admc — need NN new rxSolve calls every optimizer step — decoupling proposal generation optimization. outer phase, NN proposals ηi\eta_i drawn held fixed entire inner optimization. Given fixed proposals, evaluating NLL requires matrix operations — new rxSolve calls. proposals reweighted likelihood current Ω\Omega inner objective remains valid approximation parameters move: wi∝p(ηi∣Ω)q(ηi),μpred=∑iwif(θ,ηi),Vpred=∑iwi(f(θ,ηi)−μpred)(⋯)⊤+Σ w_i \propto \frac{p(\eta_i \mid \Omega)}{q(\eta_i)}, \qquad \mu_\text{pred} = \sum_i w_i\, f(\theta, \eta_i), \qquad V_\text{pred} = \sum_i w_i\,   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_i) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)   \bigl(\cdots\bigr)^\top + \Sigma inner optimizer therefore runs convergence negligible cost; proposals refreshed phases. Box constraints parameters progressively tightened across phases guide global convergence. decoupling makes adirmc particularly well-suited complex ODE models rxSolve expensive: total number solves scales number phases rather number optimizer steps. also robust poor starting values inner optimisation deterministic depend re-sampling. Advantages: Far fewer rxSolve calls per optimizer step admc — especially beneficial complex ODE systems expensive solves. Robust poor starting values; deterministic inner loop. AIC directly comparable admc. Limitations: Heavier per-phase overhead admc simple, well-initialised problems cheap ODE solves.

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: adgh evaluates population integrals exactly (accuracy quadrature rule) using tensor-product Gauss-Hermite grid η∼𝒩(0,Ω)\eta \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Omega). nodes ηq\eta_q weights wqw_q computed via Golub-Welsch algorithm; LηqL\eta_q applies Cholesky factor map standard-normal nodes correlated η\eta space: μpred=∑q=1Qwqf(θ,ηq),Vpred=∑q=1Qwq(f(θ,ηq)−μpred)(⋯)⊤+Σ \mu_\text{pred} = \sum_{q=1}^Q w_q\, f(\theta, \eta_q), \qquad V_\text{pred} = \sum_{q=1}^Q w_q\,   \bigl(f(\theta,\eta_q) - \mu_\text{pred}\bigr)   \bigl(\cdots\bigr)^\top + \Sigma mm nodes per dimension nηn_\eta random effects grid Q=mnηQ = m^{n_\eta} points. objective fully deterministic — MC noise — gradient clean Hessian well-conditioned. Advantages: Noise-free objective — cleaner gradient MC; reproducible across runs without fixing n_sim. AIC directly comparable admc adirmc (likelihood scale). models ≤4\leq 4 etas, QQ small (e.g. 54=6255^4 = 625) adgh substantially faster admc equivalent accuracy. Unbiased IIV magnitude (unlike FO); approximation ff. Limitations: Node count grows exponentially: 55=31255^5 = 3125, 56=156255^6 = 15625. high-dimensional IIV consider admc adirmc. inner-loop cost saving complex ODE systems (unlike adirmc); NLL evaluation runs QQ rxSolve calls.

title: Estimator comparison: adfo, admc, adgh and adirmc
text: model uses mu-referenced parameterisation (cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)), enables analytical gradient computation via sensitivity equations four estimators. See Advanced usage vignette details.
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2) library(ggplot2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID) n     <- length(ids)  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] } E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov.wt(dv_mat, method = "ML")$cov  pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  study <- list(E = E, V = V, n = n, times = times, ev = rxode2::et(amt = 100))

title: The mathematics of aggregate data modelling
text: Meta-analysis pharmacometrics means combining evidence across studies obtain parameter estimates single study support . classical approach pools individual patient data (IPD) multiple studies one dataset. scientifically ideal practically rare: data sharing agreements, proprietary restrictions, simple fact published work reports summary statistics make IPD pooling exception rather rule. Model-based meta-analysis (MBMA) addresses fitting pharmacometric models directly aggregate summaries. summaries come two distinct sources. first directly published: many clinical studies report mean concentration-time profile together variance time point, sometimes full covariance matrix. second model-derived: published pharmacometric model parameter estimates (fixed effects, random-effects variance, residual error) can used generate expected mean vector covariance matrix via simulation, without access original data. covariance structure carries information shape individual profiles variance-summary discard — two studies can identical variances time point yet different correlation structures, reflecting different sources variability. types aggregate data, whether published directly derived published model, can analysed within framework. makes full literature — competitor compounds, historical datasets, regulatory submissions — available modelling input dose selection, trial design, disease-progression modelling. aggregate data likelihood, first systematically described Välitalo (2021), provides statistical foundation MBMA: given nonlinear mixed-effects model, probability observing reported mean covariance? first R implementation admr, described van de Beek et al. (2025). admixr2 re-implements approach within nlmixr2/rxode2 ecosystem, extending new algorithmic ideas focus post.

Simulate the fitted population. We solve a plain, residual-free copy of the #    model so the band shows between-subject variability alone -- and because a #    multi-endpoint fit can only be re-solved with per-endpoint DVID/CMT tags, #    whereas this gives cp and cb directly. sim_model <- rxode2::rxode2({   cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)   qin <- exp(tqin); qout <- exp(tqout); vb <- 5   d/dt(central) <- -(cl/v1)*central - (qin/v1)*central + (qout/vb)*brain   d/dt(brain)   <-  (qin/v1)*central - (qout/vb)*brain   cp <- central / v1   cb <- brain / vb }) grid <- seq(0.25, 13, by = 0.25) sim  <- rxode2::rxSolve(sim_model,   params = theta[c("tcl", "tv1", "tqin", "tqout")],   omega  = omega, nSub = 1000L,   events = ev |> rxode2::et(grid), returnType = "data.frame")  # 3. Summarise model and data as mean +/- SD per time, per compartment. band <- function(value)   data.frame(time = sort(unique(sim$time)),              mean = tapply(value, sim$time, mean),              sd   = tapply(value, sim$time, sd))  pred <- rbind(cbind(compartment = "Plasma", band(sim$cp)),               cbind(compartment = "Brain",  band(sim$cb))) obs  <- rbind(   data.frame(compartment = "Plasma", time = plasma_times, mean = plasma_mean, sd = plasma_sd),   data.frame(compartment = "Brain",  time = brain_times,  mean = brain_mean,  sd = brain_sd))  # 4. Model (line + band) over data (points + error bars), coloured by compartment. pal <- c(Plasma = "#0072B2", Brain = "#D55E00")   # Okabe-Ito, colour-blind safe  ggplot() +   geom_ribbon(data = pred,               aes(time, ymin = mean - sd, ymax = mean + sd, fill = compartment),               alpha = 0.15) +   geom_line(data = pred,             aes(time, mean, colour = compartment), linewidth = 1) +   geom_errorbar(data = obs,                 aes(time, ymin = mean - sd, ymax = mean + sd, colour = compartment),                 width = 0.4, linewidth = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_point(data = obs,              aes(time, mean, fill = compartment),              shape = 21, colour = "white", size = 3, stroke = 0.7) +   scale_colour_manual(values = pal, breaks = c("Plasma", "Brain")) +   scale_fill_manual(values = pal, guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2),                      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.03))) +   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, NA), clip = "off") +   guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.4))) +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "Concentration (mg/L)",        title = "Fitted population prediction vs. observed data",        subtitle = "Line + band: model (mean ± SD)   ·   Points + bars: data (mean ± SD)",        colour = NULL) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(     legend.position = "top",     legend.justification = "left",     legend.margin = margin(b = 2),     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),     plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 9, margin = margin(b = 9)),     axis.title = element_text(colour = "grey25"),     axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 6)),     axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 6)),     panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),     panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),     panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),     plot.margin = margin(12, 16, 10, 12)   ) Kp_uu <- exp(theta[["tqin"]]) / exp(theta[["tqout"]]) round(Kp_uu, 2) #> [1] 0.5

title: Multiple studies
text: partition 500 examplomycin subjects two cohorts 250 compute separate aggregate statistics :
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2) library(ggplot2)  data("examplomycin") obs   <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs   <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids   <- unique(obs$ID)  dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(ids), ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] }  # Alternate subjects into two equal cohorts idx1 <- seq(1, length(ids), by = 2)   # rows 1, 3, 5, ... → cohort 1 idx2 <- seq(2, length(ids), by = 2)   # rows 2, 4, 6, ... → cohort 2  E1 <- colMeans(dv_mat[idx1, ]); V1 <- cov.wt(dv_mat[idx1, ], method = "ML")$cov; n1 <- length(idx1) E2 <- colMeans(dv_mat[idx2, ]); V2 <- cov.wt(dv_mat[idx2, ], method = "ML")$cov; n2 <- length(idx2)

title: Multiple studies
code: pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5)  ; label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10) ; label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30) ; label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10) ; label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1)  ; label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2); label("Proportional residual error SD")     eta.cl ~ 0.09     eta.v1 ~ 0.09     eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09     eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2)     q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: admixr2 fits pharmacometric PK/PD models directly aggregate-level data — observed mean vector E covariance matrix V reported per clinical study — rather requiring individual patient records. integrates nlmixr2 / rxode2 ecosystem provides four estimation backends:

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: Model-Based Meta-Analysis (MBMA) pharmacometric framework synthesising evidence across multiple clinical studies fitting shared mechanistic PK/PD model aggregate outcomes (means, variances) reported study. Unlike classical meta-analysis, pools effect estimates, MBMA preserves full pharmacometric model structure — including nonlinear dose-response, inter-individual variability, residual error — enabling principled extrapolation dose optimisation across evidence base. admixr2 fits single population model jointly studies. -study differences outcomes accounted population model structure: inter-individual variability captures subject-level spread within study, residual error absorbs remaining discrepancies. study contributes dosing regimen, observation times, sample size, shares structural variance parameters.

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
text: Individual patient data unavailable — common scenario MBMA. Published papers report means standard deviations; regulatory submissions competitive reasons prevent individual patient data sharing across companies institutions. admixr2 extracts maximum information publicly available. Leveraging literature trial design — fit mechanistic PK/PD model aggregated results existing trials, simulate new dosing regimens patient populations committing costly study. Combining evidence across heterogeneous trials — studies differ dose, formulation, population, observation schedule. admixr2 handles multi-study fits per-study dosing events time grids single shared population model. Reproducing extending published models — digitised mean concentration– time profiles figures sufficient input. individual patient data required.

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R

title: admixr2 — Aggregate Data Modelling in R
code: library(admixr2) library(rxode2) library(nlmixr2)  # 1. Compute aggregate statistics from individual data (or digitise from paper) data("examplomycin") obs    <- examplomycin[examplomycin$EVID == 0, ] obs    <- obs[order(obs$ID, obs$TIME), ] times  <- sort(unique(obs$TIME)) ids    <- unique(obs$ID) dv_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(ids), ncol = length(times)) for (i in seq_along(ids)) {   sub         <- obs[obs$ID == ids[i], ]   dv_mat[i, ] <- sub$DV[order(sub$TIME)] } E <- colMeans(dv_mat) V <- cov.wt(dv_mat, method = "ML")$cov  # 2. Define the model (standard nlmixr2 syntax) pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl     <- log(5);  label("Log clearance (L/hr)")     tv1     <- log(10); label("Log central volume (L)")     tv2     <- log(30); label("Log peripheral volume (L)")     tq      <- log(10); label("Log inter-compartmental CL (L/hr)")     tka     <- log(1);  label("Log absorption rate constant (1/hr)")     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)     eta.cl ~ 0.09; eta.v1 ~ 0.09; eta.v2 ~ 0.09     eta.q  ~ 0.09; eta.ka ~ 0.09   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl); v1 <- exp(tv1 + eta.v1)     v2 <- exp(tv2 + eta.v2); q  <- exp(tq  + eta.q)     ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka)     d/dt(depot)      <- -ka * depot     d/dt(central)    <- ka * depot - (cl/v1 + q/v1) * central + (q/v2) * peripheral     d/dt(peripheral) <- (q/v1) * central - (q/v2) * peripheral     cp <- central / v1     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  # 3. Fit fit <- nlmixr2(   pk_model, admData(), est = "admc",   control = admControl(     studies = list(examplomycin = list(       E = E, V = V, n = length(ids),       times = times, ev = et(amt = 100)     )),     n_sim = 5000L, seed = 1L   ) )  print(fit) plot(fit)

code: adfoControl(   studies = list(),   grad = c("none", "analytical", "fd", "cfd"),   algorithm = NULL,   maxeval = 500L,   ftol_rel = .Machine$double.eps^(1/2),   print = 10L,   seed = 12345L,   cores = rxode2::rxCores(),   nDisplayProgress = .Machine$integer.max,   grad_h = 1e-04,   grad_bounds = 5,   cov_h = 0.001,   cov_h_outer = .Machine$double.eps^(1/5),   covMethod = c("r", "none"),   n_restarts = 1L,   restart_sd = 0.5,   workers = 1L,   rxControl = NULL,   calcTables = FALSE,   compress = TRUE,   ci = 0.95,   sigdig = 4,   sigdigTable = NULL,   addProp = c("combined2", "combined1"),   optExpression = TRUE,   sumProd = FALSE,   literalFix = TRUE,   returnAdmr = FALSE,   ... )

title: Control settings for the FO (First-Order) estimator — adfoControl
text: studies Named list study specifications (format admControl(): E, V, n, times, ev, optional method; observations list multi-compartment fits – see admControl()). grad Gradient mode.  "none" (default) uses derivative-free BOBYQA; "analytical" uses closed-form FO gradient (requires sensitivity equations); "fd" uses forward finite differences full NLL; "cfd" uses central finite differences struct theta gradient (accurate "fd", roughly twice many NLL evaluations per step). algorithm nloptr algorithm, NULL (default) pick default matches grad: "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS" gradient, "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA" grad = "none". algorithm reported nloptr::nloptr.print.options() accepted. explicit algorithm reconciled grad: grad = "none" gradient-based algorithm (NLOPT_LD_* / NLOPT_GD_*) falls back "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA"; gradient requested derivative-free algorithm (NLOPT_LN_* / NLOPT_GN_*) turns gradient . emit message. maxeval Maximum function evaluations (default 500). ftol_rel Relative tolerance (default sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)). print Print-frequency live progress (0 = silent). seed Random seed (used restarts). cores OpenMP threads rxSolve(). Defaults rxode2::rxCores(). workers > 1 total budget, split across workers. nDisplayProgress Passed rxSolve(): show solver's text progress bar single solve exceeds many subjects. default (.Machine$integer.max) keeps clean script/vignette output; lower (e.g. 1000L) see progress long fits. grad_h Finite-difference step unpaired struct theta gradient FD Jacobian. grad_bounds Box-constraint half-width using gradients. cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). Default 1e-3. cov_h_outer Outer step scale NLL-FD Hessian. covMethod "r" computes covariance via numerical Hessian structural residual-error parameters (omega/IIV SEs computed, consistent nlmixr2 FOCEI); "none" skips . n_restarts Number optimizer restarts (1 = multi-start). restart_sd Standard deviation random perturbations initial struct thetas restart (> 1). workers Number parallel workers (mirai daemons) multi-restart (default 1 = sequential). Requires mirai package. rxControl rxode2::rxControl() object. Created automatically NULL. calcTables, compress, ci, sigdig, sigdigTable, optExpression, sumProd, literalFix Passed nlmixr2est::foceiControl() table/output machinery. addProp combined additive+proportional error parameterised nlmixr2 output tables: "combined2" (default, variance form) "combined1" (SD form). effect admixr2's estimation. returnAdmr TRUE, return plain list instead full nlmixr2 fit object. ... what: Control settings for the Gauss-Hermite (GH) quadrature estimator — adghControl
title: Control settings for the Gauss-Hermite (GH) quadrature estimator — adghControl
text: Creates control object nlmixr2(est = "adgh"). GH estimator integrates model predictions random-effects prior \(\eta \sim N(0, \Omega)\) using deterministic tensor-product Gauss-Hermite quadrature grid. unbiased IIV magnitude (unlike FO), noise-free (unlike MC), much faster MC models ~4 etas.

title: Control settings for the Gauss-Hermite (GH) quadrature estimator — adghControl
text: studies Named list study specifications (format admControl(): E, V, n, times, ev, optional method; observations list multi-compartment fits – see admControl()). n_nodes Number quadrature nodes per eta dimension (default 5). Total nodes = n_nodes^n_eta. n_nodes = 5 achieves near-exact covariance moments IIV SD ~0.5; n_nodes = 7 extends coverage SD ~0.7. models >= 5 etas node count grows steeply; consider reducing n_nodes using different estimator. grad Gradient mode. "analytical" (default) uses closed-form contractions sensitivity equations – cheapest exact. "fd" uses forward finite differences; "cfd" uses central FD. "none" uses derivative-free BOBYQA. algorithm nloptr algorithm. text: studies Named list study specifications. element list : E – observed mean vector V – observed covariance matrix variance vector (auto-detected) n – sample size times – numeric vector observation times ev – rxode2::et() dosing event table method – "cov" "var" (optional; auto-detected V) Multi-compartment (multiple observed outputs). fit several observed compartments simultaneously (e.g. plasma brain/CSF), give study observations list instead top-level E/V/times. entry one observed output output (model prediction variable, e.g. "cp" "cCSF"), times, E, V – independent fits – ev n. Pass endpoint names admData(), e.g. admData(c("cp", "cCSF")), nlmixr2 recognises every endpoint. two modes: Independent – observed output n/ev (separate experiments / subjects, e.g. plasma study brain study combined meta-analysis). outputs independent likelihood blocks aggregate -2LL sum. Joint (subjects) – outputs measured subjects. Give study shared n ev, joint covariance either study-level full matrix V (blocks observations order) per-output marginal V plus cross list cross-covariance blocks keyed "outA:outB" (length(times_A) x length(times_B); omitted pairs zero). compartments scored single MVN stacked vector shared random effects. est = "adirmc" support multiple observed outputs; use "admc", "adfo" "adgh". n_sim Number Monte Carlo samples per NLL evaluation. outer_iter Maximum inner optimiser iterations per phase. sampling Sampling method eta draws: "sobol" (Sobol, default), "halton" (Halton), "torus" (Kronecker/torus), "lhs" (Latin hypercube), "rnorm" (iid normal). algorithm nloptr algorithm string, NULL (default) pick default matches grad: "NLOPT_LD_LBFGS" gradient, "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA" grad = "none". algorithm reported nloptr::nloptr.print.options() accepted (e.g. "NLOPT_LD_MMA", "NLOPT_LN_NELDERMEAD"). explicit algorithm reconciled grad: grad = "none" gradient-based algorithm (NLOPT_LD_* / NLOPT_GD_*) falls back "NLOPT_LN_BOBYQA"; gradient requested derivative-free algorithm (NLOPT_LN_* / NLOPT_GN_*) turns gradient . emit message. maxeval Maximum number optimizer function evaluations. ftol_rel Relative function-value tolerance convergence. print Print progress every many evaluations (0 = silent). omega_expansion Inflate proposal Omega factor (>= 1). seed Random seed reproducibility. cores Number OpenMP threads rxSolve(). Defaults rxode2::rxCores(). rxSolve() parallelises subjects, main speed lever MC estimators; workers > 1 total budget, split across workers. nDisplayProgress Passed rxSolve(): solver shows text progress bar single solve exceeds many subjects. default (.Machine$integer.max) keeps bar , want scripts, vignettes logs; lower (e.g. 1000L) see solver progress long interactive fits. grad Gradient mode inner optimiser: "analytical" (default, closed-form weight-path gradient), "none" (derivative-free BOBYQA), "fd" (finite differences). Note: "sens" "cfd" available IRMC estimator. kappa_method Kappa correction method models non-mu-referenced struct thetas: "exact" (default, re-evaluates population prediction f(theta, 0) via rxSolve inner step), "linearized" (precomputes J = df/d(theta) per outer iteration using f(theta, 0) baseline — zero rxSolve per inner step), "linearized_gh" (linear approximation baseline Jacobian use Gauss-Hermite quadrature E_GH[f(theta, eta)] instead f(theta, 0) — accurate baseline IIV magnitude, still zero rxSolve per inner step). kappa_n_nodes Number GH nodes per eta dimension kappa_method = "linearized_gh" (default 5). Total quadrature points = kappa_n_nodes^n_eta. Ignored kappa methods. grad_h Step size finite-difference gradient evaluation optimization (used grad = "fd" "cfd"). default 1e-4 near optimal balance truncation error (grows h) MC noise amplification (grows 1/h) forward FD. Central FD ("cfd") slightly wider optimum around 1e-3, 1e-4 works well . cov_h Inner FD step gradient-based Hessian (used covMethod = "r" grad != "none"). gradient evaluation MC noise order sigma / cov_h; Hessian divides noise outer step, giving total noise sigma / (cov_h * cov_h_outer * |p|). cov_h = 1e-3 balances truncation error noise amplification. Increase 1e-2 Hessian non-positive definite. cov_h_outer Outer step scale numerical Hessian. actual step parameter p max(|p|, 0.1) * cov_h_outer. Applied gradient-FD Hessian (grad != "none") NLL-FD Hessian (grad = "none"). Default eps^(1/5) (~2.5e-3) larger textbook eps^(1/4) account MC noise NLL gradient evaluations; empirically matches analytical (sensitivity-equation) Hessian ground truth. Increase (e.g. 5e-3 1e-2) Hessian non-positive definite. phases Numeric vector box-constraint half-widths, one per phase. Phases progressively tighten search region. convcrit Convergence criterion: phase ends |approx - exact| < convcrit. max_worse Stop phase many consecutive worsening iterations. covMethod Covariance method: "r" (numerical Hessian structural residual-error parameters ; omega/IIV SEs computed, consistent nlmixr2 FOCEI) "none". cov_n_sim Number MC samples covariance (Hessian) step. samples reduce MC noise NLL evaluations. NLL-based Hessian (grad = "none") uses central second difference NLL Sobol sequence (CRN) every perturbed point, noise largely cancels cov_n_sim = 10000 (default) sufficient models. n_restarts Number optimization restarts. Runs parallel workers > 1. restart_sd Standard deviation structural theta perturbations restart initialisation. workers Number parallel workers multi-restart. 1 (default) runs restarts sequentially. Values > 1 run restarts pool background R processes (mirai daemons), behaves way every platform. Requires mirai package. Workers stopped automatically restart phase cores available Hessian step; fit interrupted, admStopWorkers() cleans survivors. rxControl rxode2::rxControl() object. text: Generates population mean vectors (E) covariance matrices (V) study integrating IIV distribution – either Monte Carlo (default) deterministic First-Order expansion (method = "fo", see datagenControl()).  study may specify PK/PD model (case digitising data several published studies, fit different structural model).  True parameter values taken ini() block study's model.  element returned list ready supply directly admControl(studies = ...).

title: Generate aggregate study data from (possibly different) pharmacometric models — datagen
text: studies named list study specifications.  element list : model nlmixr2-style model function ini() model() blocks.  Serves data-generating model study.  May differ studies.  Can omitted top-level default supplied via model argument. times Numeric vector observation times. ev dosing event table created rxode2::et(). n (Optional) integer sample size; stored metadata used supplying result admControl(). observations (Optional) named list generate data several observed outputs (multi-compartment). entry gives one output's output (model prediction variable, e.g. "cp"), times, optionally ev/n (inherited study otherwise). present, study result carries matching observations list per-output E/V, ready pass straight admControl(studies = ...). model Optional default model function used study supply model element.  least one model study's model must non-NULL. control datagenControl() object.

title: Generate aggregate study data from (possibly different) pharmacometric models — datagen
text: named list one element per study.  element contains: E Population mean vector times. V Population covariance matrix (length(times) x length(times); ML denominator n_sim method = "mc", analytical FO covariance method = "fo", GH weighted covariance method = "gh"). diagonal carries model's residual-error variance; generate residual-free (IIV-) moments, omit error term model. n Sample size (NA_integer_ supplied). times Observation times. ev Dosing event table. samples Raw n_sim x length(times) prediction matrix (control$return_samples = TRUE).

title: Generate aggregate study data from (possibly different) pharmacometric models — datagen
text: control = datagenControl(method = "mc") (default) population moments computed via Monte Carlo engine est = "admc": $$E_t = \bar{f}_s(\hat\theta_s, \eta_i, t)$$ $$V_{ts} = \widehat{\mathrm{Cov}}_\eta[f_{s,t}, f_{s,s'}] + \Sigma_s$$ \(f_s\) \(\hat\theta_s\) model initial estimates ini() block study \(s\), sample covariance uses ML denominator n_sim, \(\Sigma_s\) diagonal entries determined study model's residual error type (additive, proportional, log-normal). method = "fo" moments instead deterministic First-Order expansion used est = "adfo": $$E = f_s(\hat\theta_s, 0)$$ $$V = J \Omega_s J^\top + \Sigma_s, \quad J_{tj} = \partial f_{s,t}/\partial \eta_j |_{\eta = 0}$$ Jacobian \(J\) obtained sensitivity model (finite differences unavailable). natural choice design evaluation optimal design: moments fast reproducible, data-generating data-analytic models coincide, FO Hessian log-likelihood (expected information matrix) evaluated true maximum rather point MLE generated data. Note est = "adfo" always adds \(\Sigma\) predicted covariance, consistent FIM keep residual error generating model; omit residual-free (IIV-) moments genuinely want. method = "gh" moments computed deterministic Gauss-Hermite quadrature random-effects prior \(\eta \sim N(0, \Omega)\): $$E = \sum_q w_q f(\hat\theta, \eta_q), \quad V = \sum_q w_q (f_q - E)(f_q - E)^\top + \Sigma$$ \((\eta_q, w_q)\) Cholesky-scaled tensor-product GH nodes weights. Unlike FO unbiased IIV magnitude; unlike MC result noise-free exactly reproducible. Matching moments est = "adgh" makes method = "gh" natural choice optimal design estimator. Models compiled cached first use (keyed model expression digest), repeated calls multiple studies sharing model incur single compilation.

title: Generate aggregate study data from (possibly different) pharmacometric models — datagen
code: # \donttest{ library(rxode2)  pk_model <- function() {   ini({     tcl <- log(5); tv <- log(30)     prop.sd <- c(0, 0.2)     eta.cl ~ 0.09; eta.v ~ 0.04   })   model({     cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl)     v  <- exp(tv  + eta.v)     d/dt(central) <- -(cl/v) * central     cp <- central / v     cp ~ prop(prop.sd)   }) }  study_data <- datagen(   studies = list(     study1 = list(times = c(1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 24),                   ev = rxode2::et(amt = 100), n = 200L)   ),   model   = pk_model,   control = datagenControl(n_sim = 2000L) ) #>   #>   #> ℹ parameter labels from comments are typically ignored in non-interactive mode #> ℹ Need to run with the source intact to parse comments  # E and V plug directly into admControl(studies = ...) round(study_data$study1$E, 2) #>    1    2    4    8   12   24  #> 2.83 2.37 1.68 0.88 0.48 0.10  # }

title: Control parameters for datagen() — datagenControl
text: method Moment approximation used generate E V: "mc" (default) draws Monte Carlo samples IIV distribution, est = "admc"; "fo" uses deterministic First-Order expansion (mu = f(theta, 0), V = J Omega J' + Sigma), matching est = "adfo"; "gh" uses deterministic Gauss-Hermite quadrature random-effects prior, matching est = "adgh" – unbiased IIV magnitude noise-free.  Use "fo" "gh" design evaluation data-generating data-analytic models must coincide. n_sim Number Monte Carlo samples used approximate population moments. Ignored method = "fo" "gh". n_nodes Number Gauss-Hermite nodes per eta dimension method = "gh" (default 5). Total nodes = n_nodes^n_eta. Ignored "mc" "fo". sampling Quasi-random sampling method: "sobol" (default), "halton", "torus", "lhs", "rnorm". Ignored method = "fo" "gh". seed Integer seed.  Applied stochastic methods ("rnorm", "lhs"). Ignored method = "fo" "gh". cores Number rxSolve threads. return_samples Include raw n_sim x length(times) prediction matrix $samples study's output. effect method = "fo" "gh" (methods draw samples).

title: Diagnostic plots for an admixr2 fit — plot.admFit
text: "mean" – Observed vs predicted mean per study (2x2 grid). Upper row: observed predicted mean lines +/-1 SD ribbon shared y scale (black throughout). Lower row: raw residual lollipop +/-2 SE band standardised residual z-scores +/-1.96 reference lines. "cov" – Observed vs predicted (co)variance heatmaps per study (2x2 grid). Upper row shares common colour scale (blue-white-red). Lower row uses distinct diverging scales: residual (red-white-green) standardised residual (gold-white-purple). Significance stars overlaid standardised residual panel. "nll" – NLL trace per restart optimizer evaluations. Restarts coloured Okabe-Ito palette. "par" – Parameter trace per restart natural scale (struct thetas back-transformed, sigma SD, omega diagonal variance labelled V(eta.x)). Facets ordered model ini() block. Restarts coloured Okabe-Ito palette.

title: admixr2 (development version)
text: Parallel restarts now run mirai daemons. workers > 1 starts pool background R processes instead dispatching future/furrr. replaces previous fork (Unix/macOS) vs PSOCK (Windows/RStudio) split single code path behaves identically every platform, pool lives mirai compute profile never disturbs daemons user set code. furrr future longer used; mirai moves Suggests. Workers still stopped automatically restart phase (now also error/interrupt, via .exit()), cores free covariance step; admStopWorkers() remains available. Multi-compartment fitting (multiple observed outputs). study may now observe several model outputs (e.g. plasma brain/CSF) via observations list – one entry per observed output output, times, E V. Two modes (#85): Independent – output n/ev (separate experiments, e.g. literature meta-analysis); aggregate -2LL sum per-output likelihood blocks. Fit full analytical / sensitivity gradients. Joint (subjects) – outputs measured subjects, shared n/ev joint covariance given either study-level full V per-output marginal V plus cross list cross-covariance blocks. Scored single MVN stacked vector shared random effects full analytical gradient three estimators (number compartments; assembled joint covariance checked positive-definiteness). Supported est = "admc", "adfo" "adgh"; datagen() generates multi-output aggregate data plot() renders one panel set per compartment. Pass endpoint names admData(), e.g. admData(c("cp", "cCSF")). est = "adirmc" support multiple observed outputs. nDisplayProgress control argument every estimator (admControl(), adfoControl(), adghControl(), adirmcControl()), passed rxSolve() calls drive fitting. sets many subjects single solve must exceed solver shows text progress bar. default (.Machine$integer.max) keeps bar , longer leaks scripts, logs rendered vignettes; lower (e.g. 1000L) watch progress long interactive fits.

title: admixr2 (development version)
text: solver progress bar longer appears covariance/gradient batches. internal rxSolve() calls already suppressed , covariance batched-gradient solves admc hard-coded low nDisplayProgress (1000), bar printed chunk exceeded 1000 solves. solves now honour new nDisplayProgress control argument (default ). Hard-coded numeric constants model’s model({}) block (e.g. fixed brain volume vb <- 5, common PBPK/CNS models) longer zeroed. admixr2 used hand-fill every model parameter set 0, clobbering constant’s default producing NA/non-finite objective (e.g. qout / vb divide--zero). now supplies parameters varies lets rxSolve() fill rest model’s defaults, constants covariate defaults keep value. adgh now computes gradients non-mu-referenced (unpaired) structural thetas. unpaired-parameter set derived eta-indexed struct_eta_idx, always empty thetas silently received zero gradient; now uses struct-indexed struct_has_eta.

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-02

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: New estimator est = "adgh": deterministic Gauss-Hermite quadrature random-effects prior, configured via adghControl(). objective noise-free (Monte Carlo draws), analytical gradient exact, unbiased IIV magnitude. models ~4 random effects fastest exact estimator (#65). datagen() gains FO-approximated population moments (method = "fo", matching est = "adfo") design evaluation optimal-design work (#56). adirmcControl(kappa_method = "linearized_gh"): GH-averaged kappa baseline IRMC inner loop. admClearCache() prunes session-level compiled-model cache (#10). Control objects now accept nloptr algorithm; default chosen gradient mode, grad/algorithm reconciled automatically (#70).

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: Fix infinite recursion (“evaluation nested deeply” / “node stack overflow”) aborted first fit R session covariance matrix requested (covMethod = "r"). Accessing ui$simulationModel left self-referential compiled-model object ui$meta, nlmixr2’s ui-cloning fit assembly traverse. admixr2 now clears transient artifact .admLoadModel(), keeping ui canonical state nlmixr2 expects. Affected four estimators (adfo/admc/adgh/adirmc) (#81). Use ML denominator (1/n_sim) consistently MC gradient kernels, matching NLL (#48). Fix parallel multi-restart dispatch fork/PSOCK, fix adirmc multi-restart (#45). Guard non-positive predicted variance diagonal-NLL paths (#57). Correct FO diagonal omega gradient scaling, plus assorted plot, output-variable detection, caching, worker-serialization fixes.

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: Add Gauss-Hermite sections across vignettes fix pkgdown reference index documentation site builds (#79).

title: admixr2 0.2.0
text: Declare minimum versions imported rxode2 (>= 5.1.2) nlmixr2est (>= 6.0.1), suggested nlmixr2 (>= 5.0.0) (used examples tests).

title: admixr2 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-02 Initial release. Monte Carlo estimator (est = "admc") via admControl(). Iterative Reweighting Monte Carlo estimator (est = "adirmc") via adirmcControl(). Analytical CRN gradient sensitivity equations (grad = "sens"). Multi-restart parallelism via furrr/future. Diagnostic plots: observed vs predicted mean/covariance, NLL trace, parameter trace. traceplot() support: admixr2 fits populate standard parHistData slot, nlmixr2 traceplot() generic works natively (best restart, natural scale, burn-marker). Integrates nlmixr2/rxode2 ecosystem.
